searchDocs.title: Network​
searchDocs.content: </> interface Network { Namespace: string-- The unique identifier for this network channel. Folder: Folder? -- The folder containing the RemoteEvent and RemoteFunction. Event: RemoteEvent? -- The RemoteEvent used for one-way communication. Function: RemoteFunction? -- The RemoteFunction used for two-way (request/response) communication. _dispatch: DispatchClientFn | DispatchServerFn-- Internal dictionary for routing incoming actions. }

searchDocs.title: DispatchServerFn​
searchDocs.content: </> type DispatchServerFn = {[string]: ( player: Player , data: any? ) → any?} A dictionary mapping action names to server-side callback functions.

searchDocs.title: DispatchClientFn​
searchDocs.content: </> type DispatchClientFn = {[string]: (data: any?) → any?} A dictionary mapping action names to client-side callback functions.

searchDocs.title: Recipient​
searchDocs.content: </> type Recipient = Player | {Player } | "all" Defines the target(s) for a server-to-client push. Can be a single Player, an array of Players, or the string "all" to broadcast to everyone.

searchDocs.title: NetworkPayload​
searchDocs.content: </> interface NetworkPayload { data: any?-- The actual data to send across the network. options: NetworkOptions?-- Optional configuration for how the request should be handled. }

searchDocs.title: NetworkOptions​
searchDocs.content: </> interface NetworkOptions { debug: boolean?-- If true, prints warning and success messages to the output for debugging. timeout: number?-- The maximum time (in seconds) to wait for a response before cancelling. retry: number?-- The number of times to retry a failed request. usePromise: boolean?-- If true, the request will immediately return a Promise instead of yielding the thread. }

searchDocs.title: set​
searchDocs.content: </> Networker.set( namespace: string-- The unique identifier for this network channel. ) → Networker-- The networker instance. Initializes a new Network namespace. If one already exists, it returns the cached instance.

searchDocs.title: send​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the client. Client </> Networker.send( self: Networker, actionName: string,-- The action to trigger on the server. payload: Types.NetworkPayload?-- Optional data and configuration options. ) → () Dispatches a one-way event to the server. Does not yield or wait for a response.

searchDocs.title: request​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the client. Client This is a yielding function. When called, it will pause the Lua thread that called the function until a result is ready to be returned, without interrupting other scripts. Yields </> Networker.request( self: Networker, actionName: string,-- The action to request from the server. payload: Types.NetworkPayload?-- Optional data, retries, timeout, and Promise configuration. ) → Promise | any?-- Returns a Promise if usePromise is true, otherwise returns the raw data. Yields the current thread to request data from the server. Automatically handles timeouts, retries, and optional Promise wrapping.

searchDocs.title: listen​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the client. Client </> Networker.listen( self: Networker, actionName: string,-- The action to listen for. fn: (data: any?) → any?-- The callback function to execute when the event is received. ) → () Registers a callback to listen for incoming one-way events from the server.

searchDocs.title: push​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the server. Server </> Networker.push( self: Networker, actionName: string,-- The action to trigger on the client(s). recipient: Types.Recipient,-- The target Player, array of Players, or "all". payload: Types.NetworkPayload?-- Optional data and configuration options. ) → () Pushes data to a specific recipient, a list of recipients, or broadcasts to "all" clients. Does not yield.

searchDocs.title: on​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the server. Server </> Networker.on( self: Networker, actionName: string,-- The action to handle. fn: ( player: Player , data: any? ) → any?-- The callback function to execute. Returns data back to the client if invoked via request. ) → () Registers a callback to handle incoming requests or events from a client.
